metadata.description: Maps ReasonDx topics to Radiopaedia cases for imaging integration
metadata.usage_note: Links to case pages with staged reveal, not static labeled images

chest.teaching_focus: Lobar vs interstitial patterns, air bronchograms, silhouette sign
chest.order_reasoning: First-line for suspected respiratory infection; low cost, fast, widely available
chest.management_impact: Confirms diagnosis, guides antibiotic selection (lobar vs atypical pattern), identifies complications

chest.teaching_focus: Visceral pleural line, deep sulcus sign (supine), mediastinal shift in tension
chest.order_reasoning: Upright CXR preferred; tension pneumothorax is a clinical diagnosis — don't delay treatment for imaging
chest.expected_findings: Visible visceral pleural line, absent lung markings peripherally, deep sulcus sign if supine
chest.management_impact: Size determines observation vs chest tube; tension = immediate decompression

chest.teaching_focus: Meniscus sign, costophrenic blunting, ultrasound for thoracentesis guidance
chest.order_reasoning: CXR detects >200mL; lateral decubitus confirms free-flowing; US guides safe drainage
chest.expected_findings: Costophrenic angle blunting, meniscus sign, layering on decubitus
chest.management_impact: Light's criteria (exudate vs transudate) guides etiology workup

chest.teaching_focus: Bilateral opacities, dependent consolidation, ground-glass opacities
chest.order_reasoning: CXR for diagnosis per Berlin criteria; CT if atypical or not improving
chest.expected_findings: Bilateral opacities not explained by effusions or atelectasis
chest.management_impact: Confirms diagnosis, assesses severity, guides ventilator strategy

chest.teaching_focus: Filling defects in pulmonary arteries, RV strain signs, Hampton hump, Westermark sign on CXR
chest.order_reasoning: CTPA is gold standard after positive Wells/D-dimer; consider V/Q if contrast contraindicated
chest.expected_findings: Intraluminal filling defect, RV/LV ratio >1 indicates strain
chest.management_impact: Massive PE = thrombolytics; submassive = anticoagulation ± monitoring; saddle PE = ICU

chest.teaching_focus: Apical cavitary lesions (reactivation), hilar lymphadenopathy (primary), miliary pattern
chest.order_reasoning: Screening and diagnosis; CT if CXR equivocal or extrapulmonary suspected
chest.management_impact: Determines isolation needs, guides sputum collection, distinguishes active vs latent

cardiac.teaching_focus: ST elevation patterns by territory, reciprocal changes, posterior MI
cardiac.order_reasoning: ECG within 10 minutes of presentation; cath lab for STEMI
cardiac.expected_findings: ST elevation (STEMI), ST depression/T-wave inversion (NSTEMI), new LBBB
cardiac.management_impact: STEMI → immediate PCI (<90 min); NSTEMI → risk stratify for timing of cath

cardiac.teaching_focus: Cephalization, Kerley B lines, bat-wing edema, cardiomegaly; echo for EF
cardiac.order_reasoning: CXR for acute decompensation; echo for EF classification (HFrEF vs HFpEF)
cardiac.expected_findings: Cardiomegaly (CTR >0.5), pulmonary vascular redistribution, pleural effusions
cardiac.management_impact: EF determines medication regimen (GDMT for HFrEF); CXR response guides diuresis

cardiac.teaching_focus: Intimal flap, true vs false lumen, Stanford classification
cardiac.order_reasoning: CTA is first-line; widened mediastinum on CXR raises suspicion but is neither sensitive nor specific
cardiac.expected_findings: Intimal flap separating true/false lumen, branch vessel involvement
cardiac.management_impact: Type A → emergent surgery; Type B → medical management unless complicated

cardiac.teaching_focus: Pericardial effusion, RV diastolic collapse, IVC plethora, respiratory variation
cardiac.order_reasoning: Bedside echo in unstable patients; formal echo for hemodynamic assessment
cardiac.expected_findings: Circumferential effusion, RV collapse in diastole, swinging heart
cardiac.management_impact: Hemodynamically significant → pericardiocentesis; stable → workup etiology

cardiac.teaching_focus: Valve morphology, regurgitant jets, gradient severity, chamber dimensions
cardiac.order_reasoning: TTE is first-line; TEE for better visualization of mitral valve, prosthetics, endocarditis
cardiac.expected_findings: Stenosis (elevated gradients), regurgitation (jet area/vena contracta), calcification
cardiac.management_impact: Severity grading determines surgical timing; serial echo for surveillance

cardiac.teaching_focus: Vegetations, abscess, valve perforation, new regurgitation
cardiac.order_reasoning: TTE first; TEE if TTE negative but high suspicion (modified Duke criteria)
cardiac.expected_findings: Oscillating vegetation on valve, paravalvular abscess, new regurgitation
cardiac.management_impact: Vegetation size >10mm increases embolic risk; abscess = surgical indication

abdominal.teaching_focus: Dilated appendix >6mm, periappendiceal fat stranding, appendicolith
abdominal.order_reasoning: CT with IV contrast is gold standard in adults; US first in children and pregnant patients
abdominal.expected_findings: Appendiceal diameter >6mm, wall enhancement, fat stranding, appendicolith
abdominal.management_impact: Uncomplicated → surgery; perforation/abscess → may need drainage first; antibiotics alone in select cases

abdominal.teaching_focus: Gallstones, wall thickening >3mm, pericholecystic fluid, Murphy sign on US
abdominal.order_reasoning: US is first-line (fast, no radiation); HIDA if US equivocal (cystic duct obstruction)
abdominal.expected_findings: Cholelithiasis, gallbladder wall thickening, pericholecystic fluid, CBD dilation if cholangitis
abdominal.management_impact: Cholecystitis → cholecystectomy; cholangitis → ERCP for decompression; Tokyo criteria guide severity

abdominal.teaching_focus: Pancreatic enlargement, peripancreatic fluid, necrosis (non-enhancing areas), Balthazar grade
abdominal.order_reasoning: CT NOT needed for diagnosis (clinical + lipase); CT at 72-96h if not improving to assess necrosis
abdominal.expected_findings: Pancreatic enlargement, peripancreatic stranding, fluid collections, necrosis
abdominal.management_impact: Necrosis determines ICU vs floor; infected necrosis → drainage; pseudocyst → observation vs drainage

abdominal.teaching_focus: Dilated proximal loops >3cm, transition point, small bowel feces sign, closed-loop
abdominal.order_reasoning: CT with IV contrast (oral not needed); XR shows air-fluid levels but CT localizes transition point
abdominal.expected_findings: Dilated proximal loops with decompressed distal bowel, transition point, cause (adhesion, hernia, mass)
abdominal.management_impact: Partial → NGT + NPO + observation; complete/closed-loop → surgery; strangulation signs = emergent OR

abdominal.teaching_focus: Active contrast extravasation, vascular malformations, tagged RBC scan timing
abdominal.order_reasoning: Upper GI bleed → EGD within 24h; lower → colonoscopy; CTA if massive/unstable
abdominal.expected_findings: Active extravasation on CTA, bleeding source on endoscopy
abdominal.management_impact: Active bleed on CTA → IR embolization or surgery; stable → endoscopic intervention

abdominal.teaching_focus: Free air (perforation), free fluid, inflammatory changes, vascular compromise
abdominal.order_reasoning: CT is the workhorse; upright CXR for free air; US first for RUQ/pelvic/pediatric
abdominal.expected_findings: Depends on etiology — free air, obstruction, inflammation, vascular occlusion
abdominal.management_impact: Free air → OR; SBO with ischemia → OR; inflammatory → medical vs surgical based on source

abdominal.teaching_focus: Nodular liver surface, caudate hypertrophy, ascites, portal hypertension signs, varices
abdominal.order_reasoning: US for screening and surveillance; triple-phase CT/MRI for HCC screening (LI-RADS)
abdominal.expected_findings: Shrunken nodular liver, splenomegaly, ascites, portal vein dilation, varices
abdominal.management_impact: HCC screening q6mo in cirrhosis; MELD score for transplant; TIPS for refractory ascites

abdominal.teaching_focus: Hepatomegaly, periportal edema (starry sky), gallbladder wall thickening
abdominal.order_reasoning: US to assess liver parenchyma and rule out biliary obstruction; Doppler for vascular patency
abdominal.expected_findings: Hepatomegaly, starry sky pattern, periportal tracking, gallbladder wall edema
abdominal.management_impact: Distinguishes hepatitis from biliary obstruction; assesses chronicity

neuro.teaching_focus: Hyperdense vessel sign, loss of grey-white differentiation, ASPECTS score, CTA occlusion
neuro.order_reasoning: Non-contrast CT to EXCLUDE hemorrhage (within minutes); CTA for LVO; perfusion for mismatch
neuro.expected_findings: Early: hyperdense MCA sign, insular ribbon sign; Late: hypodense territory
neuro.management_impact: Hemorrhage excluded → tPA if <4.5h; LVO on CTA → thrombectomy if <24h with mismatch

neuro.teaching_focus: Hyperdense blood in cisterns/sulci, Fisher grade, aneurysm on CTA
neuro.order_reasoning: Non-contrast CT >95% sensitive in first 6h; LP if CT negative but high suspicion; CTA for aneurysm
neuro.expected_findings: Hyperdense blood in basal cisterns, sylvian fissure, interhemispheric fissure
neuro.management_impact: Fisher grade predicts vasospasm; aneurysm location determines clip vs coil; Hunt-Hess guides prognosis

neuro.teaching_focus: CT to rule out mass/midline shift before LP; MRI shows meningeal enhancement
neuro.order_reasoning: CT before LP only if: altered mental status, immunocompromised, papilledema, focal neuro deficit, seizure
neuro.expected_findings: CT usually normal in uncomplicated meningitis; MRI shows leptomeningeal enhancement
neuro.management_impact: Don't delay antibiotics for imaging; CT clears for safe LP; MRI shows complications (abscess, ventriculitis)

neuro.teaching_focus: CT for acute (hemorrhage, mass); MRI epilepsy protocol for structural causes
neuro.order_reasoning: CT in ED for first seizure/new-onset; MRI epilepsy protocol for recurrent/unexplained
neuro.expected_findings: CT: may show mass, hemorrhage, calcification; MRI: hippocampal sclerosis, cortical dysplasia, tumor
neuro.management_impact: Structural lesion = consider surgery; normal MRI = medical management; status epilepticus imaging after stabilization

neuro.teaching_focus: Red flags (thunderclap, worst ever, focal deficit); normal CT doesn't rule out everything
neuro.order_reasoning: CT for thunderclap/acute worst-ever headache; MRI for recurrent/atypical; MRA/MRV for vascular causes
neuro.expected_findings: SAH on CT, mass lesion, cerebral venous thrombosis on MRV, idiopathic intracranial hypertension
neuro.management_impact: Thunderclap + negative CT → LP for xanthochromia; mass → neurosurgery; IIH → acetazolamide

neuro.teaching_focus: Ventricular dilation, transependymal flow, Evans index
neuro.order_reasoning: CT for acute (fast); MRI for chronic/NPH workup; US for neonatal (open fontanelle)
neuro.expected_findings: Dilated ventricles, Evans index >0.3, periventricular transependymal edema
neuro.management_impact: Acute = emergent EVD; communicating = shunt; NPH = large volume LP trial → shunt

musculoskeletal.teaching_focus: Femoral neck vs intertrochanteric, Garden classification, occult fracture on MRI
musculoskeletal.order_reasoning: AP pelvis + lateral hip first; MRI if XR negative but high clinical suspicion (occult fracture)
musculoskeletal.expected_findings: Cortical disruption, trabeculae disruption, Shenton line break
musculoskeletal.management_impact: Displaced femoral neck → arthroplasty; non-displaced → fixation; intertrochanteric → ORIF

musculoskeletal.teaching_focus: Bone marrow edema, periosteal reaction, sequestrum, involucrum, soft tissue abscess
musculoskeletal.order_reasoning: MRI is most sensitive/specific; XR shows changes only after 10-14 days; bone scan if MRI contraindicated
musculoskeletal.expected_findings: T1 low signal (edema replacing marrow), T2/STIR high signal, enhancement, periosteal reaction
musculoskeletal.management_impact: Guides biopsy location, surgical planning, duration of antibiotics (6 weeks IV typical)

musculoskeletal.teaching_focus: This is primarily a CLINICAL diagnosis — imaging can delay treatment
musculoskeletal.order_reasoning: Do NOT wait for imaging; compartment pressures if clinical exam equivocal (>30mmHg or within 30 of diastolic)
musculoskeletal.expected_findings: Imaging is secondary — muscle edema on MRI; elevated pressure on manometry
musculoskeletal.management_impact: Fasciotomy within 6 hours; delay = irreversible muscle necrosis and contracture

renal_gu.teaching_focus: Stone location, size, hydronephrosis, perinephric stranding
renal_gu.order_reasoning: Non-contrast CT is gold standard (>95% sensitivity); US if pregnant/pediatric; KUB for follow-up
renal_gu.management_impact: <5mm → conservative (90% pass); 5-10mm → possible intervention; >10mm → likely needs procedure; infected → emergent

renal_gu.teaching_focus: Three-phase CT urogram for complete evaluation; US for initial screening
renal_gu.order_reasoning: CT urogram is definitive; US for initial evaluation especially in young patients
renal_gu.expected_findings: Stone, mass, cyst, hydronephrosis, urothelial thickening
renal_gu.management_impact: Mass → urology referral; stone → size-based management; glomerular hematuria → nephrology

renal_gu.teaching_focus: Wedge-shaped hypoperfusion, perinephric stranding, abscess, obstructing stone
renal_gu.order_reasoning: CT if not improving on antibiotics at 48-72h, or if obstruction suspected
renal_gu.expected_findings: Renal enlargement, striated nephrogram, perinephric fat stranding, abscess formation
renal_gu.management_impact: Abscess >3cm → drainage; obstructing stone + infection → emergent decompression (urology emergency)

renal_gu.teaching_focus: Absent blood flow (torsion), intratesticular mass, hydrocele, epididymal changes
renal_gu.order_reasoning: US with color Doppler is first-line; do NOT delay surgery for imaging if torsion clinically obvious
renal_gu.expected_findings: Absent or decreased flow (torsion), heterogeneous mass (tumor), enlarged epididymis (epididymitis)
renal_gu.management_impact: Torsion → OR within 6h for salvage; mass → tumor markers + orchiectomy; epididymitis → antibiotics

obstetric.teaching_focus: Empty uterus with positive beta-hCG, adnexal mass, free fluid
obstetric.order_reasoning: TVUS when beta-hCG >discriminatory zone (1500-2000); serial hCG if below
obstetric.expected_findings: Empty uterus, adnexal mass/ring of fire, free fluid in cul-de-sac
obstetric.management_impact: Stable + <3.5cm + no fetal heartbeat → methotrexate; ruptured/unstable → surgery

obstetric.teaching_focus: Retroplacental hematoma, placental edge separation; US may be NORMAL in abruption
obstetric.order_reasoning: Clinical diagnosis primarily; US has limited sensitivity (~25-50%); MRI if stable and indeterminate
obstetric.expected_findings: Retroplacental collection (hyper→hypoechoic over time), placental thickening
obstetric.management_impact: Severe = emergent delivery; mild with preterm = expectant management with monitoring

pediatric.teaching_focus: Target sign (transverse), pseudokidney sign (longitudinal), trapped mesenteric fat
pediatric.order_reasoning: US is first-line in peds (no radiation); air/contrast enema is both diagnostic and therapeutic
pediatric.expected_findings: Target/doughnut sign on transverse, pseudokidney on longitudinal
pediatric.management_impact: Air enema reduction (85-90% success); surgery if peritonitis, perforation, or failed reduction

pediatric.teaching_focus: Pyloric muscle thickness >3mm, length >15mm, target sign
pediatric.order_reasoning: US is definitive; no radiation; performed after rehydration
pediatric.expected_findings: Muscle thickness >3mm, channel length >15mm, failure of pylorus to open
pediatric.management_impact: Correct metabolic alkalosis first, then pyloromyotomy

pediatric.teaching_focus: Transition zone, rectosigmoid ratio reversal, delayed evacuation at 24h
pediatric.order_reasoning: Contrast enema shows transition zone; rectal suction biopsy is gold standard for diagnosis
pediatric.expected_findings: Narrow aganglionic segment with dilated proximal colon, rectosigmoid index <1
pediatric.management_impact: Surgical pull-through procedure; may need initial diverting colostomy

pediatric.teaching_focus: RDS (ground glass + air bronchograms), TTN (fluid in fissures), MAS (patchy infiltrates), pneumothorax
pediatric.order_reasoning: CXR is primary tool; distinguishes surgical (CDH, TEF) from medical causes
pediatric.expected_findings: RDS: diffuse ground glass; TTN: perihilar streaking; MAS: hyperinflation with patchy opacities
pediatric.management_impact: RDS → surfactant; TTN → supportive; CDH → intubate, do NOT bag-mask, surgery

pediatric.teaching_focus: Bowel loops in thorax, mediastinal shift, absent stomach bubble in abdomen
pediatric.order_reasoning: Prenatal US for diagnosis; postnatal CXR confirms; NGT placement shows stomach position
pediatric.expected_findings: Gas-filled bowel in hemithorax, mediastinal shift to contralateral side, hypoplastic lung
pediatric.management_impact: Stabilize (gentle ventilation, NO bag-mask), NGT decompression → surgical repair after stabilization
